title: Developer vows to present more ‘options’ for Davis Square tower project amid neighborhood opposition
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Developer Copper Mill hosts public meeting, commits to presenting new options for a proposed 26-story tower in Somerville's Davis Square.
The proposed 502-unit tower includes 126 affordable units, but faces criticism for not fitting neighborhood character and size concerns.
Construction could impact local businesses, including The Burren; Copper Mill promises no resident or public parking in the design.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for his 2 nephews with down syndrome
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Steven Ponte, 37, from Somerville, Mass., is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in memory of his nephew Sebastian.
Ponte's nephews, Sebastian and Zachary, were diagnosed with Down syndrome shortly after birth and received support from the MDSC.
Sebastian passed away from respiratory issues; Ponte aims to honor him and support Down syndrome families through the race.

title: Boston firefighter, National Guard member reportedly injured in strike that killed 6 U.S. soldiers
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Six U.S. soldiers were killed in an Iranian drone strike in Kuwait, injuring a Boston firefighter and National Guard member.
Army National Guard Major Mark Garcia, also a Boston firefighter, is recovering in Germany from injuries sustained in the attack.
The Boston Firefighters IAFF Local 718 union expressed gratitude for Garcia's safety and service, anticipating his return to the city.

title: Lowell murder trial delayed after lead State Police investigator allegedly causes fatal crash while driving drunk
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Lowell murder retrial delayed indefinitely after State Police investigator involved in a fatal crash in December 2023.
Sgt. Scott Quigley, linked to the murder case, allegedly drove drunk, striking a van; passenger died later.
Middlesex District Court's delay decision considers ongoing investigation into Quigley's actions and trial fairness concerns.

title: ​Bruins’ playoff hopes might rest on Jeremy Swayman’s shoulders for this stretch run
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jeremy Swayman has a .906 save percentage, showing improvement and resilience despite a challenging season for the Bruins' defense.
The Bruins are currently in the second Wild Card spot, with a record of 36-22-6 and 78 points.
Manager Marco Sturm may need to rely heavily on Swayman as playoffs approach, especially given Joonas Korpisalo's recent struggles.

title: Watch: Body cam shows Patriots party at downtown Boston restaurant
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Boston police released body camera footage showing hookahs and strippers at a Patriots party in Estella restaurant.
Officers reported finding three nude or partially nude women and unauthorized after-hours activities at the party.
The restaurant owner blamed Patriots players for the situation; the venue received a three-day license suspension.

title: 4 things to know about new Patriots safety Kevin Byard
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Kevin Byard signs a one-year, $9 million deal with the New England Patriots, bringing his experience as a safety.
Byard has 36 interceptions in his 10-year NFL career, leading the league in 2025 with seven interceptions for the Bears.
Byard has never missed a game due to injury in his NFL career, showcasing his durability over 10 seasons.

title: Man resentenced in 2011 slaying of Dorchester teen
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Nyasani Watt sentenced to 14-17 years for 2011 killing of Dorchester teen, eligible for parole due to credited time.
Victim Jaivon Blake, 16, was fatally shot by Watt, while another teen was injured; incident involved gang violence.
Original convictions overturned in 2024 due to right to counsel violation; Watt pleaded guilty to reduced charges.

title: Samuel Adams launches new ‘Our City. Our Beer.’ variety pack inspired by Boston sports stars
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Samuel Adams releases 'Our City. Our Beer.' variety pack inspired by Boston athletes Derrick White, Garrett Crochet, Julian Edelman, and Zdeno Chára.
The variety pack includes four themed beers: White Ale, Crochet's Pale Ale, Julian Edel-Brau Lager, and Big Z Pilsner.
A public launch party at Banners Kitchen & Tap in Boston is scheduled for April 4 with athlete appearances expected.

title: Boston Marathon race director to run with his 3 kids — and astronaut Sunita Williams
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Dave McGillivray, 71, will run his 54th Boston Marathon with three of his children: Max, Luke, and Elle McGillivray.
Retired astronaut Sunita Williams joins McGillivray, having previously run the marathon on the International Space Station in 2007.
The McGillivray family is fundraising for various causes, including the Justice Health Initiative and the Finish Strong Foundation.
